reviewNotes: Preferred fewer stronger authoring targets over duplicating storage concepts likely covered in lesson 02.
Storage partitioning, GPT/MBR, and quick/full format should be merged or reconciled against existing storage/file-system curriculum.
Secure Boot and DiskPart are not strong standalone authoring targets from this transcript alone.
Normalize transcript typos and terminology before authoring: PXE, multiboot, zero-touch, reimaging, GUID.

conceptDecisions.title: Bootable USB operating system installation
conceptDecisions.reason: Bootable USB media is a practical OS installation method with enough source support to author as a focused install-media concept.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: PXE network boot
conceptDecisions.reason: PXE is defined and tied directly to network-based OS installation; it deserves authoring with light enrichment for DHCP/TFTP dependencies.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: ISO image
conceptDecisions.reason: ISO images are clearly defined as disc images and connected to bootable installation and virtualization use.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Internet-based operating system installation
conceptDecisions.reason: The lesson mentions internet-based installs across several OS families, but exact mechanisms vary and should be verified before standalone authoring.
mustCover: internet install as an installation source

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Multiboot systems
conceptDecisions.reason: Multiboot has enough evidence and exam relevance, especially with partition planning and OS selection during boot.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Clean install
conceptDecisions.reason: Clean install is a core installation type with clear destructive impact and strong relationship to backup practices.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: In-place upgrade
conceptDecisions.reason: The transcript clearly contrasts in-place upgrade with clean install and explains what is preserved.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Image deployment
conceptDecisions.reason: Image deployment is well supported as an enterprise-scale deployment method and should move forward as a deployment concept.
mustCover: basic imaging tool examples as enrichment

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Zero-touch deployment
conceptDecisions.reason: Zero-touch deployment is sufficiently described and valuable, but should be linked carefully to unattended installation rather than duplicated.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Remote network installation from local share
conceptDecisions.reason: This is better handled as part of network-based OS installation/PXE coverage unless the curriculum later needs a separate share-based install concept.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Recovery partition
conceptDecisions.reason: Recovery partitions are clearly defined, tied to hidden partitions, and useful for troubleshooting/reinstallation.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Repair installation
conceptDecisions.reason: Repair installation is a distinct troubleshooting recovery method and belongs in software troubleshooting foundations.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Third-party driver loading during OS installation
conceptDecisions.reason: The lesson provides a clear use case: installer lacks storage or network drivers, requiring third-party driver loading.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: Disk partition

conceptDecisions.title: Windows volume
conceptDecisions.reason: Windows volume terminology is best handled as a subtopic of disk partitions/formatting rather than a standalone object from this lesson.

curriculumDecision.reason: Placement accepted or adjusted based on whether the concept belongs with OS installation, troubleshooting, security, or existing storage/file-system coverage.

conceptDecisions.title: GPT partition style
conceptDecisions.reason: GPT likely already exists from storage/file-system work and should not be duplicated; normalize GUID terminology during merge.

conceptDecisions.title: UEFI firmware

conceptDecisions.title: Secure Boot

conceptDecisions.title: Quick format

gapReview.reason: The installation lesson assumes boot process and firmware boot-order basics.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Create or link a boot process/boot order prerequisite concept.

gapReview.reason: PXE needs A+ appropriate DHCP/TFTP/service dependency context.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Enrich PXE authoring with basic dependency notes.

gapReview.reason: Destructive install and formatting tasks require backup awareness.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Link destructive installation topics to backup/data preservation concepts.

gapReview.reason: The transcript uses GID wording; authoring must normalize to GUID/GPT.
Format behavior has storage-type and OS-version caveats.
gapReview.recommendedAction: Keep A+ baseline simple and add reviewed caveats where appropriate.

globalReviewNotes: Accepted most OS installation method concepts for authoring because they are directly supported and curriculum-relevant.
Merged most storage partition/formatting concepts to reduce duplicate Knowledge Objects and preserve the knowledge-first architecture.
The review output is not a Knowledge Object export and does not create learner-facing explanations, quizzes, or flashcards.
